originalBehavior: The relaunched Emoji Simulator opens directly into a grid editor with reset, pause, speed, step, and draw controls above the canvas.
localStatus: The local route keeps the original control bar, grid mount, and editor sidebar structure with only metadata and footer cleanup at the shell level.
notes: The local shell still mounts the upstream editor runtime without wrapping the controls in a different app framework.
Public provenance is limited to the shared footer reference link to the live route.
evidence: The current plan treats reset, pause, speed, step, and draw controls as parity-critical behavior for this route.
Local smoke coverage exercises the control bar directly under the shipped /interactive-explanation/sim/ path.

originalBehavior: Readers can draw states onto the grid, edit state descriptions and rules, and reshape the world proportions from the editor sidebar.
localStatus: The editor sidebar remains upstream-driven, including brush cycling, rule editing, and proportion controls, while creator or promo copy was removed from the tail of the panel.
notes: The local cleanup avoided rewriting the editor data model or the DOM it generates.
Only the visible helper copy around save or export was neutralized for the public route.
evidence: The plan explicitly called out draw or edit mode as parity-critical.
Local smoke coverage checks brush changes and sidebar-driven state under the shipped route.

originalBehavior: The tool supports loading built-in preset models from query parameters and exposes visible example links inside the default description text.
localStatus: Preset loading remains upstream-driven through ?s=..., while visible example text in the shipped JSON descriptions now uses local links and local cross-route references.
notes: The default forest preset still acts as the landing example, but its visible links no longer point to the upstream domain.
The Schelling preset now points to the local ../polygons/ route because that interactive is already shipped locally.
evidence: The current plan explicitly called out model selection or model-loading behavior as part of parity coverage.
Local smoke coverage opens the ?s=schelling preset and verifies the description text uses the localized cross-link.

originalBehavior: The relaunched tool can generate local compressed share URLs and export the current model as JSON in a new tab.
localStatus: The original compressed ?lz=... save flow and JSON export behavior remain intact, with public helper copy stripped down to neutral instructions.
notes: The local route still computes save links from window.location so they stay inside the GitHub Pages-style subpath.
The public route no longer advertises TinyURL, GitHub promo copy, or Patreon-style credits.
evidence: The current plan requires local model loading and export behavior where supported by the shipped repo.
Local smoke coverage verifies the saved link contains a local ?lz=... URL and that export still opens a JSON data URL.

originalBehavior: The published bundle expects to run as a standalone static site rooted at /sim/.
localStatus: The local shell normalizes canonical and Open Graph URLs to the current page, preserves relative asset loading under the nested subpath, and adds the shared footer as the only original-page reference surface.
notes: The route still loads its assets relatively from the shipped folder instead of relying on root-path hosting.
The shared footer is injected at document level only and does not alter the grid or editor runtime.
evidence: The route now loads under /interactive-explanation/sim/ with local canonical metadata.
Audit coverage passes with the shipped sim route included in the public-surface sweep.
